name: PyPI Warehouse API
what_it_does: The PyPI (Python Package Index) Warehouse API provides JSON-based access to Python package metadata including version listings, release files, dependency requirements, classifiers, and download statistics. The primary interface is per-package JSON endpoints (pypi.org/pypi/{package}/json and pypi.org/pypi/{package}/{version}/json). A separate XML-RPC API exists for legacy use and the Simple Index (PEP 503/691) is used by pip for package resolution.
use_cases: Fetching Python package metadata and available versions for dependency analysis or Dependabot-style update tools
Resolving the latest stable version of a package for automated requirements.txt or pyproject.toml updates
Inspecting package classifiers, license, and maintainer info for compliance and due-diligence workflows
Retrieving release file hashes for reproducible environment builds and supply chain verification
Checking download statistics to assess package popularity and community health
not_for: Actually installing Python packages (use pip, uv, or poetry)
Private package hosting (use PyPI's paid plans, Gemfury, or a self-hosted DevPI instance)
High-volume bulk metadata crawling (use the BigQuery public dataset or the RSS feed instead)
best_when: An agent needs to look up Python package metadata, resolve version information, or verify package integrity as part of dependency management or security analysis.
avoid_when: You need to install packages, host private packages, or do bulk analysis (use BigQuery dataset).

auth.notes: All read operations (package JSON endpoints, Simple Index) require no authentication. Package upload (via twine or the upload API) requires an API token (replaces username+password as of 2023). API tokens are generated from pypi.org account settings and can be scoped to a specific project or all projects. Tokens are passed as HTTP Basic auth with username `__token__`. No OAuth flow.

pricing.free_tier_limits: All reads are free and unauthenticated. Publishing public packages is free.
pricing.paid_tiers: None. PyPI is operated by the Python Software Foundation.
pricing.notes: PyPI is a free community service. There are no paid tiers. CDN costs are covered by sponsorship. Rate limits exist but are not aggressively enforced for well-behaved clients.

performance.rate_limits: No hard published rate limits for the JSON API. The Simple Index and package downloads are CDN-backed (Fastly). Aggressive crawling may trigger IP-based throttling.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: The JSON package endpoint returns 404 with a minimal HTML error for unknown packages (not JSON), which breaks naive JSON parsers. The upload API returns XML-RPC-style errors. Errors are not consistently structured across different PyPI endpoints. Rate limit responses are generic HTTP 429s without detailed guidance.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: GET operations on package endpoints are fully idempotent and highly cacheable. Package uploads are not idempotent — uploading the same filename with different content is rejected; uploading the same file is a no-op on newer PyPI. Version deletion (via the website) cannot be done via API; only yanking is supported via twine/API.
known_agent_gotchas: The 404 response for unknown packages returns HTML, not JSON — agents must handle Content-Type checking before JSON parsing
Package names are normalized by PyPI (hyphens and underscores are interchangeable, case-insensitive) — store the canonical name from the metadata, not user input
The `info.requires_dist` field uses PEP 508 dependency specifiers which require a parser to interpret properly
The `releases` key in the JSON response is a dict keyed by version string — iteration order is not guaranteed to be version-sorted
PyPI's yanking mechanism (not deletion) means yanked releases still appear in the JSON but with `yanked: true` — agents must filter for dependency resolution
The XML-RPC API is legacy and deprecated; agents should use only the JSON endpoints and the Simple Index (PEP 691 JSON format)
Download statistics are NOT included in the package JSON; they require a separate call to pypistats.org (unofficial but widely used)

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. API tokens can be scoped to a specific project (recommended for publish workflows). Mandatory 2FA for critical projects (top 1% of downloads) is enforced. Trusted publishing (OIDC from GitHub Actions) is now supported as a keyless alternative to tokens. PyPI has faced supply chain attacks (typosquatting) — agents should normalize and verify package names carefully.

score_rationale: The PyPI JSON API is clean and fast for the happy path — fetching package metadata for known packages requires just a single unauthenticated GET. However, 404 responses returning HTML (not JSON) is a significant agent gotcha, dependency resolution requires understanding PEP 508 specifiers, and download stats are entirely external. The project-scoped API tokens and trusted publishing support are welcome improvements. Strong for read-only metadata lookups; workable for publish workflows with proper error handling.
